sources.title: Conclusion on the peer review of the pesticide risk assessment of the active substance difenoconazole
notes: EFSA peer review used as governed public toxicological-reference-value context for difenoconazole.
Dietary MCP uses this as review-oriented evidence and method provenance, not as a native enforcement database.

sources.title: Modification of the existing maximum residue levels for difenoconazole in wheat and rye
notes: EFSA reasoned opinion used as governed residue-review context for difenoconazole in cereal matrices.
Included for review-oriented monitoring and analytical-context linkage, not as a native MRL engine.

sources.title: Setting of import tolerances for difenoconazole in various crops
notes: EFSA import-tolerance opinion used as governed public analytical-enforcement and matrix-scope context for difenoconazole in citrus fruits, tree nuts, dry peas, and soya beans.
Retained for monitoring-oriented provenance and method linkage, not as a native import-tolerance engine.

sources.title: Difenoconazole (JMPR 2013 evaluation)
notes: Official JMPR evaluation used as governed Codex-global residue and acute-reference-value context for difenoconazole.
Included to preserve EFSA versus JMPR acute-reference-value differences for review-oriented evidence handling.

sources.title: Conclusion on the peer review of the pesticide risk assessment of the active substance glyphosate
notes: Current EFSA peer review supporting a glyphosate ADI of 0.5 mg/kg bw/day and ARfD of 1.5 mg/kg bw.
Dietary MCP uses this as review-oriented evidence and method provenance.

sources.title: Conclusion on the peer review of the pesticide risk assessment of the active substance glyphosate
notes: Historical EFSA peer review that established the 0.5 mg/kg bw glyphosate ARfD.
Retained as superseded evidence after the 2023 peer review established an ARfD of 1.5 mg/kg bw.

sources.title: Glyphosate (JMPR 2016 evaluation)
notes: Official JMPR evaluation used as governed Codex-global residue and reference-value context for glyphosate.

sources.title: Conclusion regarding the peer review of the pesticide risk assessment of the active substance imidacloprid
notes: EFSA peer review establishing the 0.08 mg/kg bw imidacloprid ARfD.
The value was retained in EFSA's 2019 MRL consumer risk assessment and was not unconditionally superseded by the 2013 recommendation.

sources.title: Scientific Opinion on the developmental neurotoxicity potential of acetamiprid and imidacloprid
notes: Primary EFSA scientific opinion recommending a 0.025 mg/kg bw/day ADI and 0.025 mg/kg bw ARfD for acetamiprid, and a 0.06 mg/kg bw ARfD for imidacloprid.
The imidacloprid ADI of 0.06 mg/kg bw/day was considered adequately protective; the 0.06 mg/kg bw ARfD remained a recommendation rather than an unconditional replacement.

sources.title: Review of the existing maximum residue levels for imidacloprid according to Article 12 of Regulation (EC) No 396/2005
notes: EFSA's consumer risk assessment used the 0.08 mg/kg bw ARfD derived in 2008.
This later assessment demonstrates that the 2013 recommendation of 0.06 mg/kg bw did not unconditionally supersede the 0.08 mg/kg bw context.

sources.title: Conclusion on the peer review of the pesticide risk assessment for bees for the active substance imidacloprid
notes: EFSA peer review used as governed public toxicological-reference-value context for imidacloprid.

sources.title: Imidacloprid (JMPR 2001 evaluation)
notes: Official JMPR evaluation used as governed Codex-global residue context for imidacloprid.

sources.title: Conclusion on the peer review of the pesticide risk assessment of the active substance acetamiprid
notes: EFSA peer review used as governed public toxicological-reference-value context for acetamiprid.

sources.title: Acetamiprid (JMPR 2011 evaluation)
notes: Official JMPR evaluation used as governed Codex-global residue context for acetamiprid.

sources.title: GB 2763-2026 National Food Safety Standard - Maximum Residue Limits for Pesticides in Food
sources.organization: Agricultural Product Quality and Safety Center, Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Affairs, China
notes: Official published standard text for GB 2763-2026.
The NHC announcement states that the standard took effect on 2026-03-01.
